title: Mayotte barrier reef — thermal-stress bleaching (2010, 2016, 2024)
summary: Mayotte's ~1,500 km2 double-barrier lagoon (SW Indian Ocean) bleached in the 2010, 2016 (3rd global event, strongest since 1998) and 2024 (4th global event) marine heatwaves. This POC drives a parametric bleaching trigger from NOAA Coral Reef Watch Degree Heating Weeks (DHW) over the reef, calibrates it with a reef-resilience coefficient, and backtests the payouts against observed bleaching for 2009-2024. The real DHW caught all three documented events (hit rate 1.0) with one false positive (2019); the payout-vs-severity gap (2016 was the worst-observed year but only moderate heat) is the basis risk a Discovery audit exists to quantify.

sources.label: NOAA Coral Reef Watch (5km DHW / CoralTemp)
Obura et al. 2019 — 2016 WIO bleaching (Coral Reefs)
Parc naturel marin de Mayotte (OFB) — reef status
